questions.meaning: Words given at the back of the chapter

questions.sample_sentence: I was anxious about my Dhrupad recital because my teacher said the judges would be very strict.

questions.meaning: concrete containers for rubbish or waste
questions.sample_sentence: The Chennai Corporation placed new disposal units along Greams Road so people would stop littering near our school.

questions.meaning: dirt that has accumulated on a surface
questions.sample_sentence: After playing cricket all afternoon at school, my white uniform was covered in grime and Amma made me wash it immediately.

questions.meaning: curious; eager to find out things
questions.sample_sentence: My cousin Vishwak is so inquisitive that he asked Patri Satish Kumar at least ten questions about the mridangam after the concert.
Even the meanest little tea stall near Mylapore temple serves the most amazing filter coffee you will ever taste.

questions.meaning: thin, high-pitched voice like a reed instrument
questions.sample_sentence: The old flower seller outside Kapaleeshwarar Temple called out to us in a reedy voice, offering fresh jasmine garlands.

questions.meaning: Other important words from the story

questions.sample_sentence: My cricket bat looked so shabby after two years of constant use that Appa finally agreed to buy me a new one.
On the way to Marina Beach, we passed a dilapidated old warehouse that looked like it could collapse at any moment.
After bowling twenty overs during the Sishya inter-house cricket match, I was completely exhausted and could barely walk.

questions.meaning: spider webs, especially old dusty ones
questions.sample_sentence: When we opened our storeroom during Diwali cleaning, we found cobwebs covering all the old boxes in the corner.
After bursting crackers on Diwali night, the walls of our balcony were covered in a thin layer of black soot.
When our family arrived at Chennai Central station for our vacation, the coolies rushed forward offering to carry our heavy suitcases.
Whenever Sastha Catering Services delivers food for our family function, Amma still insists on making the chapatis herself at home.
Nothing beats drinking hot filter coffee from a steel tumbler and davara on a rainy Chennai morning.
The frangipani tree near our school gate on Greams Road fills the whole entrance with its sweet fragrance every morning.
For the Sishya Christmas carnival, we decorated the stalls with bright orange marigolds and colourful streamers.
My friend Suvasana showed me how to draw a perfect hibiscus flower for our art project at school.
Our classroom was covered in silver and gold tinsel when we were preparing for the Sishya Christmas carnival.
When Patri Satish Kumar arrived for the concert at Mylapore, the organizers welcomed him with a beautiful garland of roses.
Every evening, Amma lights joss sticks near the puja room and the fragrance spreads through our entire house.
I watched the cook at Sastha Catering knead the dough with expert hands, preparing hundreds of rotis for the wedding.
After making biryani for the whole family, I had to scour the heavy vessel until every grain of burnt rice came off.
Yohann let out a loud grunt as he smashed a powerful forehand during his tennis match at the club.
We spotted Appa's Way2top course signboard near Tidel Park and felt proud seeing it displayed so prominently.
Walking along the pavement on Greams Road after school, we stopped at a street vendor for a quick snack.
The sweet shop in T. Nagar had gleaming showcases filled with rows of colourful Diwali sweets and snacks.
The lights at Chepauk stadium glittered like stars as the CSK players walked out for the evening IPL match.
Our school organized a charity cricket match to raise money for an orphan shelter near Mylapore.
My cousin Prahlad and I ran along the platform at Chennai Central, trying to find our coach before the train left.
During our family vacation to Ooty, I sent Yohann a funny postcard showing a picture of the toy train.
When the Swiggy delivery man brought our idiappam order in the pouring rain, Amma gratefully gave him a generous tip.
When it suddenly started raining at Marina Beach, we took shelter under a fisherman's overturned boat.
After my bad fever last monsoon, I felt like an invalid for a whole week and could not even attend Dhrupad practice.
In the story we read at Sishya, the drunkard wasted all his money and had nothing left to feed his family.
The sweltering Chennai heat in May makes it impossible to practice cricket outdoors, so we stay indoors and watch IPL instead.
